scenario: Persist state to localStorage with React state sync

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: SSR crash (no window), cross-tab desync, JSON types lost, key changes don't refetch

v2_mid_level.improvements: SSR-safe lazy init, JSON serialization, try/catch
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No storage event listener for cross-tab, no quota error handling

v3_senior.code: useEffect(()=>{const h=(e)=>{if(e.key===key&&e.newValue)setV(JSON.parse(e.newValue))};window.addEventListener('storage',h);return()=>window.removeEventListener('storage',h)},[key])
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Storage event syncs tabs, quota errors caught, custom serializer/deserializer params

v4_staff.code: function useLocalStorage<T>(key:string,init:T,opts?:{serialize?:(v:T)=>string;deserialize?:(s:string)=>T}){const sub=useCallback((cb:()=>void)=>{window.addEventListener('storage',cb);return()=>window.removeEventListener('storage',cb)},[]);const get=()=>{try{const r=localStorage.getItem(key);return r?opts?.deserialize?.(r)??JSON.parse(r):init}catch{return init}};return useSyncExternalStore(sub,get,()=>init)}
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: useSyncExternalStore eliminates tearing in concurrent React, SSR snapshot, generic typing

v5_principal.code:
// Schema-validated, versioned, migration-capable. Use Zod for runtime validation. Version field allows breaking changes without losing user data.
function useLocalStorage<T>(key:string,schema:ZodSchema<T>,version:number,migrate?:(old:any,fromV:number)=>T){...}
v5_principal.the_realization: Local storage is a persistence layer — treat it like a database. Schemas, migrations, and versioning matter when your app evolves. Junior treats it as a string bag.

where_most_engineers_stop: v3 — adds storage event but doesn't think about React 18 concurrent tearing
when_to_use_which_version: v2 for prototypes, v4 for production apps, v5 when schema will evolve

scenario: Check if username is taken as user types

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Network spam on every keystroke, race conditions (old response overwrites new), no debounce, no loading state

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Race condition still possible if fast network beats slow one, no abort on unmount, no error UI

v3_senior.code: useEffect(()=>{if(!debounced)return;const c=new AbortController();fetch('/check?u='+debounced,{signal:c.signal}).then(r=>r.json()).then(setAvail).catch(e=>{if(e.name!=='AbortError')setErr(e)});return()=>c.abort()},[debounced])
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: AbortController prevents race conditions, cleanup on unmount, distinguishes abort from real errors

v4_staff.code: const {data,isLoading,error}=useQuery({queryKey:['username',debounced],queryFn:({signal})=>checkUsername(debounced,signal),enabled:debounced.length>=3,staleTime:60_000})
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: TanStack Query handles caching (repeated checks free), staleTime prevents re-checks, signal handled automatically, isLoading state built-in

v5_principal.code:
// Optimistic with rollback + offline support. Combine with form library for validation choreography.
const {mutate}=useMutation({mutationFn:checkAndReserve,onMutate:async(name)=>{await qc.cancelQueries(['username',name]);qc.setQueryData(['username',name],{available:false,reserved:true});return{name}},onError:(_,_v,ctx)=>qc.setQueryData(['username',ctx.name],{available:true})})
v5_principal.the_realization: Async validation isn't a debounce problem — it's a distributed systems problem: ordering, cancellation, cache coherence, race conditions. Forms libraries solve this category, not 'I'll add a useEffect'.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — adds debounce, ships, gets flaky bug reports
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum for production, v4 if you have React Query, v5 for reservation flows

scenario: User can click items to select; shift-click selects range

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Toggle missing, shift-click missing, ctrl-click missing, keyboard inaccessible

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No keyboard support, no focus visible, no screen reader announcement

v3_senior.code: <li role='option' aria-selected={sel.has(id)} tabIndex={focused===id?0:-1} onKeyDown={e=>{if(e.key===' '||e.key==='Enter')toggle(id);if(e.key==='ArrowDown')focusNext()}}>
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: WAI-ARIA listbox pattern, roving tabindex, keyboard parity with mouse

v4_staff.code:
// Extract to headless hook + composable parts
const {getListboxProps,getOptionProps,selection}=useListbox({items,onSelectionChange,multiple:true,selectionMode:'multiple'})
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Logic separated from rendering (react-aria style), works with any visual layer, testable in isolation, composes with virtualization

v5_principal.code:
// Selection is a state machine: idle → focusing → selecting → range-selecting. Model it. Add transactions for undo.
const machine=createMachine({states:{idle:{on:{CLICK:'selecting'}},selecting:{on:{SHIFT_CLICK:'range_selecting',CTRL_CLICK:'toggling'}}}})
v5_principal.the_realization: Selection looks like a UI problem but it's a state-machine problem with accessibility and undo as first-class concerns. Most lists ship buggy because engineers don't model the states.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — mouse works, ships, accessibility bug filed in Q3
when_to_use_which_version: v3 always, v4 for design systems, v5 for complex apps (file manager)

scenario: Drawer that slides up from bottom on mobile, can be dragged to dismiss

v1_junior.code: <div className='fixed bottom-0 transform translate-y-full data-[open=true]:translate-y-0 transition'>
v1_junior.what_still_breaks: No drag, no swipe-to-dismiss, no body scroll lock, no safe-area inset, no focus management

v2_mid_level.code:
// Add Framer Motion drag
<motion.div drag='y' dragConstraints={{top:0,bottom:0}} onDragEnd={(_,info)=>{if(info.offset.y>100)onClose()}}>
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Drag conflicts with internal scroll, no velocity threshold, no rubber-banding

v3_senior.code: const y=useMotionValue(0);const opacity=useTransform(y,[0,300],[1,0]);// Velocity-based dismiss: if(info.velocity.y>500||info.offset.y>200)dismiss(); Body scroll lock via Radix Dialog or react-remove-scroll. Safe-area: pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)].
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Velocity-aware physics, backdrop fade tied to drag, safe-area aware, focus trap on open

v4_staff.code:
// Inner scroll vs drag: detect scroll position. If scrolled to top AND dragging down, treat as drag. Else treat as scroll. Vaul library does this.
const canDrag=scrollRef.current?.scrollTop===0&&direction==='down'
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Handles scroll-vs-drag conflict (the hardest part), snap points (peek/full), nested sheets

v5_principal.code:
// Treat as platform abstraction: iOS sheet, Android bottom sheet, desktop dialog. Same API, different physics per platform.
<Sheet snapPoints={['25%','50%','100%']} platform='auto' onSnap={...}>
v5_principal.the_realization: Bottom sheets are deceptively complex: gestures, physics, scroll coordination, safe areas, platform conventions. The reason Vaul exists is that getting this right takes months.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — ships, gets bug 'can't scroll inside drawer'
when_to_use_which_version: v3 for simple drawers, v4+ use Vaul/library — don't reinvent

scenario: Table filters persist in URL, sharable links

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Filter lost on refresh, can't share URL, no back/forward navigation

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Updates URL on every keystroke (history pollution), multi-filter coordination painful, typed values lost

v3_senior.code: const debounced=useDebounce(localFilter,300);useEffect(()=>{setParams(p=>{p.set('q',debounced);return p},{replace:true})},[debounced])
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Debounced URL updates, replace=true avoids history pollution, separate local + URL state

v4_staff.code:
// Typed schema for params with Zod, derive types
const schema=z.object({q:z.string().optional(),page:z.coerce.number().default(1),sort:z.enum(['name','date']).default('name')});const {q,page,sort}=schema.parse(Object.fromEntries(params))
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Type-safe URL state, defaults handled, parse errors don't crash, easy to add params

v5_principal.code:
// nuqs / TanStack Router with typed params. URL becomes the single source of truth, components subscribe by key.
const [q,setQ]=useQueryState('q',parseAsString.withDefault(''));const [page,setPage]=useQueryState('page',parseAsInteger.withDefault(1))
v5_principal.the_realization: URL is application state. Treating it as such gives you free shareable links, back-button works, deep linking, server-rendering all align. Most apps put state in React only and lose all of these.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — URL syncs but UX feels janky
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum, v4 if no library, v5 for complex apps

scenario: Type-ahead search suggesting results from API

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Race conditions, no keyboard nav, no ARIA, no debounce, dropdown stays after blur

v2_mid_level.code: const [q,setQ]=useState('');const debounced=useDebounce(q,200);useEffect(()=>{const c=new AbortController();fetch('/sug?q='+debounced,{signal:c.signal})...;return()=>c.abort()},[debounced]);// onBlur close, arrow keys to move highlight
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No ARIA combobox pattern, blur-then-click on suggestion bug, no recent items

v3_senior.code:
{items.map((it,i)=>
  • )}
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: WAI-ARIA combobox pattern, aria-activedescendant for keyboard, onMouseDown not onClick (fires before blur)

v4_staff.code:
// Use Downshift / cmdk / Ariakit — these solve the 47 edge cases. Customize via render props.
const {getInputProps,getMenuProps,getItemProps,highlightedIndex}=useCombobox({items,onInputValueChange:debouncedFetch})
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Leverages battle-tested library, customize visuals, focus on business logic (caching, ranking)

v5_principal.code:
// Search is a product. Add: recent items, fuzzy local + server, command palette (cmd+k), result ranking, analytics. cmdk + Algolia/Meilisearch + LLM reranker.
<Command><Command.Input/><Command.List><Command.Group heading='Recent'/><Command.Group heading='Results'/></Command.List></Command>
v5_principal.the_realization: Comboboxes are inputs; search is a product. Engineers who treat 'search' as 'a debounced fetch' miss the entire UX category: empty states, recents, ranking, scopes, analytics, fuzzy matching.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — ships, has accessibility audit failures
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum, v4 for design systems, v5 for product-grade search

scenario: Upload large files reliably, resume on network failure

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Times out on 100MB+, no progress, no retry, no validation, network drop = restart

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Network drop still requires full restart, no chunking, no parallel uploads

v3_senior.code:
// Chunked upload
async function upload(file){const id=await initUpload(file.name,file.size);const CHUNK=5*1024*1024;for(let i=0;i<file.size;i+=CHUNK){await fetch(`/up/${id}/${i}`,{method:'PUT',body:file.slice(i,i+CHUNK)})}await finalizeUpload(id)}
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Chunked uploads survive network blips (just retry that chunk), server tracks received chunks, can resume

v4_staff.code:
// tus.io protocol or AWS S3 multipart. Parallel chunks, MD5 verification, automatic resume.
const upload=new tus.Upload(file,{endpoint:'/up',retryDelays:[0,1000,3000,5000,10000],onProgress,onSuccess})
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Standard protocol (tus), parallel chunks, integrity verification, automatic retry with backoff

v5_principal.code:
// Treat as system: upload service with worker queue, virus scan, transcoding pipeline, signed URLs, expiry. Frontend = orchestrator.
const {data:signedUrl}=useMutation(()=>api.requestUpload({size,mime}));uploadDirectToS3(file,signedUrl);api.notifyUploadComplete(signedUrl.id)
v5_principal.the_realization: File uploads aren't a form field — they're a pipeline: chunking, resume, validation, virus scan, processing, CDN distribution. The frontend's job is to orchestrate, not to bear the bytes.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — works for demo, fails for users with bad networks
when_to_use_which_version: v3 for medium files, v4+ for production

scenario: Mobile-friendly image viewer with pinch zoom and pan

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: No zoom, no pan, no double-tap-to-zoom, no rotate

v2_mid_level.code: // CSS transform with state const [scale,setScale]=useState(1);setScale(s=>s+e.deltaY*-0.01)}/>
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No touch gestures, no pan, no bounds, zoom origin wrong

v3_senior.code:
// Pointer events + pinch detection
useGesture({onPinch:({offset:[s]})=>setScale(s),onDrag:({offset:[x,y]})=>setPos([x,y])},{target:ref,pinch:{from:[scale,0],bounds:{min:1,max:4}}})
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Touch pinch works, drag pan, bounded zoom, libraries like @use-gesture/react

v4_staff.code:
// Origin-correct zoom: zoom around finger/cursor position. Pan bounds adjust with zoom. Momentum on release.
const zoomAt=(x,y,delta)=>{const newScale=scale+delta;setPos(p=>[(p[0]-x)*newScale/scale+x,(p[1]-y)*newScale/scale+y]);setScale(newScale)}
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Zooms around pointer (not center), pan bounds prevent showing background, momentum, double-tap zoom

v5_principal.code: // Use react-zoom-pan-pinch / yet-another-react-lightbox. Custom Image component with srcset (low-res placeholder → high-res on zoom). LQIP + progressive decode. {src=>(<>)}
v5_principal.the_realization: Image viewers are touch-physics problems. The math (zoom origin, pan bounds, momentum) is what makes them feel native. Resolution-switching by zoom level is what makes them fast.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — wheel zooms but mobile is broken
when_to_use_which_version: v3 for galleries, v4+ for photo apps, library always

scenario: Drag nodes within tree to reparent or reorder

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: No drop position indicator, no nesting, no expansion on hover, no keyboard

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No auto-expand on hover, no scroll while dragging, no parent-into-child prevention

v3_senior.code:
// Auto-expand on hover (800ms), scroll near edges, validate no cycles
useEffect(()=>{if(hoverInside){const t=setTimeout(()=>expand(target),800);return()=>clearTimeout(t)}},[hoverInside]);if(isAncestor(dragged,target))return;// invalid drop
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Auto-expand, edge scroll, cycle prevention, drop indicator visualizes intent

v4_staff.code:
// dnd-kit with sortable tree. Keyboard support via screen-reader announcements.
<DndContext sensors={[useSensor(PointerSensor),useSensor(KeyboardSensor)]} accessibility={{announcements:{onDragMove:({over})=>`Drop at ${over.id}`}}}>
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Keyboard parity with mouse, screen-reader announcements, sensor abstraction, virtualization-compatible

v5_principal.code:
// CRDT-backed tree. Multi-user concurrent reordering. Yjs Y.Array + position keys (LSEQ/fractional indexing). Pessimistic vs optimistic.
const posBetween=(a,b)=>generateFractionalIndex(a,b)
v5_principal.the_realization: Tree DnD is a distributed-ordering problem when multi-user. Fractional indexing prevents conflicts. Most engineers solve for one user and get destroyed by concurrent edits.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — three zones work, cycles cause crashes
when_to_use_which_version: v3 for single-user, v4 always, v5 for multi-user

scenario: Push notifications via WebSocket, badge counter, mark read

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: No reconnect, no auth, no offline replay, no read state, no badge sync across tabs

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Missed messages during disconnect, no auth refresh, single tab

v3_senior.code:
// Cursor-based replay: track last_seen_id, on reconnect fetch missed via REST
ws.onopen=async()=>{const missed=await fetch(`/notifs?since=${lastSeenId}`);setNotifs(n=>[...n,...missed])}
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: No message loss on reconnect, cursor pagination, JWT refresh hook

v4_staff.code:
// BroadcastChannel for cross-tab, SharedWorker holds single WS
const bc=new BroadcastChannel('notifs');bc.onmessage=e=>setNotifs(...);// Only one tab connects, others receive via channel
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: One WS per browser (not per tab), SharedWorker, cross-tab badge sync, SSE fallback

v5_principal.code:
// Notifications are a domain. Multi-channel (in-app, email, push, SMS), preferences per type, digest/throttle, i18n. Backend: outbox pattern. Frontend: render-agnostic store.
const {unread,markRead,preferences}=useNotifications({channel:'in-app'})
v5_principal.the_realization: Notifications start as a UI widget and end as a multi-channel delivery system with preferences, throttling, and analytics. The hard parts are guarantees (no loss, no duplicates) and UX (don't annoy).

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — reconnects but loses messages
when_to_use_which_version: v3 for serious products, v4 for multi-tab UX, v5 for SaaS

feature_concept: Token refresh with concurrent request handling
scenario: Access token expires; refresh transparently without re-fighting concurrent requests

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: If 10 requests fire at once with expired token, 10 refresh calls happen, race conditions

v2_mid_level.code:
// Refresh-in-progress flag
let refreshing=null;async function refresh(){if(!refreshing)refreshing=fetch('/refresh').finally(()=>refreshing=null);return refreshing}
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Concurrent requests still fail their first attempt, no queue

v3_senior.code:
// Queue requests during refresh
let refreshing=null;const queue=[];async function authFetch(url){if(refreshing)await refreshing;const r=await fetch(url,{headers:{Authorization:`Bearer ${token}`}});if(r.status!==401)return r;if(!refreshing){refreshing=doRefresh().finally(()=>refreshing=null)}await refreshing;return fetch(url,{headers:{Authorization:`Bearer ${token}`}})}
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: In-flight requests await ongoing refresh, no duplicate refresh calls, retries with new token

v4_staff.code:
// Move tokens to httpOnly cookies, use refresh token rotation, CSRF protection via SameSite=Strict + double-submit
// Frontend never sees access token. Server middleware handles refresh.
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: XSS can't steal tokens (httpOnly), refresh rotation invalidates leaked tokens, no client-side complexity

v5_principal.code:
// OAuth 2.1 + PKCE + DPoP. Bound tokens (sender-constrained). Session revocation propagates via revocation list / token introspection.
// Frontend: silent renew via iframe or refresh endpoint with proof-of-possession.
v5_principal.the_realization: Token refresh isn't about retry logic — it's about cryptographic binding, revocation propagation, and threat models (XSS, CSRF, replay). Junior solves the timing; principal solves the trust.

where_most_engineers_stop: v3 — works but tokens still in localStorage (XSS-vulnerable)
when_to_use_which_version: v3 for legacy, v4 for new apps, v5 for high-value targets

feature_concept: Dark mode with system preference + user override
scenario: Respect OS dark mode, allow user override, persist choice

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: FOUC (flash of wrong theme), doesn't respect OS, lost on refresh

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: FOUC still happens (effect runs after render), system change while open ignored

v3_senior.code:
// Inline script in <head> sets class before React hydrates
<script>{`const t=localStorage.getItem('theme')||(matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:dark)').matches?'dark':'light');document.documentElement.classList.add(t)`}</script>;matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:dark)').addEventListener('change',e=>!stored&&setDark(e.matches))
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: No FOUC (script runs before paint), live system updates, three-state (light/dark/system)

v4_staff.code:
// CSS variables + data-theme attribute. Component code references var(--bg). Theme switch = attribute change. Works with SSR.
:root[data-theme='dark']{--bg:oklch(20% 0 0)}
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Theme-agnostic components, CSS-only switching (no React re-render), easy to add new themes

v5_principal.code:
// Theme is a token system. Primitives (color.gray.900) → semantic (surface.primary) → component (button.bg). Mode switches semantic→primitive mapping. Style Dictionary / Tokens Studio.
// Multi-brand: brand × mode × density × motion preferences.
v5_principal.the_realization: Dark mode is the visible tip of a token-system iceberg. Real design systems have primitives, semantics, density, motion, contrast modes — and theme is just one axis. Hard-coding 'if dark' doesn't scale.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — works but flashes on every reload
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum, v4 for design systems, v5 for multi-brand

scenario: Unauthed user hits protected route; after login, return to original

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Loses original URL, after login goes to home

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Lost on refresh (state in location is in-memory), query params lost, no nested route protection

v3_senior.code: <Navigate to={`/login?next=${encodeURIComponent(location.pathname+location.search)}`} replace/>;// In login: navigate(searchParams.get('next')||'/')
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Survives refresh (URL-based), full path preserved, replace=true prevents back-button loop

v4_staff.code:
// Loader-based protection (React Router 6.4+ / TanStack Router): redirect happens before render
loader:async()=>{const u=await getUser();if(!u)throw redirect('/login?next='+location.pathname);return u}
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: No flash of protected content, data loaded with auth check, errors handled at route level

v5_principal.code:
// Auth is a system: route-level loaders, RSC for SSR auth, edge middleware redirects (zero JS), refresh tokens, session validation. Whitelist redirects to prevent open-redirect attacks.
const safeNext=isWhitelisted(next)?next:'/'
v5_principal.the_realization: Auth gating isn't a component — it's a layer. Edge → middleware → loader → component. Earlier the check, faster the UX and stronger the security. And `next` is a redirect attack vector if you don't whitelist.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — works locally, loses state on refresh
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum, v4 for SSR apps, v5 for production

scenario: Backend returns errors; show actionable message to user

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Useless message, blocks UI, no retry, no error tracking

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Shows raw 'Network request failed', no recovery, no i18n

v3_senior.code:
// Map error codes to friendly messages
const messages={NETWORK:'Check your connection',AUTH_EXPIRED:'Please log in again',VALIDATION:'Please check your input'};const msg=messages[e.code]||'Something went wrong'
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Error codes mapped to friendly text, i18n-ready, retry button on retryable errors

v4_staff.code:
// Discriminated union error types with type guards. Sentry capture with breadcrumbs. Distinguish user errors from system errors.
type AppError={kind:'user';message:string;field?:string}|{kind:'system';code:string;traceId:string}|{kind:'network';retryable:true}
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Typed errors, structured logging with trace correlation, user-vs-system distinction drives UX (toast vs full error page)

v5_principal.code:
// Error budget thinking: SLOs define acceptable error rates. Errors are signals. Result types eliminate throws. Errors carry remediation hints.
type Result<T,E>={ok:true;value:T}|{ok:false;error:E&{remediation:Action[]}}
v5_principal.the_realization: Errors are product. They're the moment users decide to abandon. Junior shows 'error'; principal designs the recovery flow, instruments the funnel, and treats errors as a UX surface with budgets, not exceptions.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — toast shows raw message
when_to_use_which_version: v3 always, v4 for production, v5 for SaaS

scenario: Detect when LCP gets worse in production

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Not reported, no per-page tracking, no regression detection

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No P75/P95, no segmentation (device/connection), no attribution

v3_senior.code: import {onLCP} from 'web-vitals';onLCP(({value,id,navigationType,attribution})=>{sendBeacon('/rum',{lcp:value,nav:navigationType,element:attribution.element,url:attribution.url})})
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: web-vitals library handles edge cases (bfcache, tab visibility), attribution identifies which element caused LCP, sendBeacon survives unload

v4_staff.code:
// Aggregate at edge, alert on P75 regression. Segment by device/country/route. Correlate with deploys.
// Frontend: capture detailed RUM with sampling. Backend: time-series DB, regression detection.
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Statistical thinking (P75 not mean), segmentation, deploy correlation, alert on regression not absolute

v5_principal.code:
// Performance is a feature with SLOs. LCP budget per route. CI blocks PRs that regress P75 LCP. RUM correlates with revenue.
// Synthetic + real user, calibrated baselines, automated bisection.
v5_principal.the_realization: Performance monitoring isn't a dashboard — it's a feedback loop into engineering culture. SLOs, budgets, CI gates, deploy correlation. Without that, LCP numbers are vanity metrics.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — data flows but nothing acted on
when_to_use_which_version: v3 always, v4 for serious products, v5 for revenue-critical sites

scenario: React to viewport size for conditional rendering

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: SSR crash, resize fires constantly, no MQL

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: SSR hydration mismatch (initial false), tearing in concurrent React

v3_senior.code: function useMediaQuery(q:string){const get=useCallback(()=>matchMedia(q).matches,[q]);const sub=useCallback((cb:()=>void)=>{const mql=matchMedia(q);mql.addEventListener('change',cb);return()=>mql.removeEventListener('change',cb)},[q]);return useSyncExternalStore(sub,get,()=>false)}
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: useSyncExternalStore prevents tearing, SSR snapshot returns false (consistent), proper cleanup

v4_staff.code:
// Prefer CSS container queries / responsive utilities over JS media queries. Use JS only for conditional component logic, not styling.
// Pair with React 19 useDeferredValue for expensive re-layouts.
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Recognizes when CSS is the right tool (styling), JS only for behavior changes — fewer re-renders, better INP

v5_principal.code:
// Container queries (CSS) for component-local responsiveness. Media queries for global breakpoints only. Element queries via ResizeObserver where CQ unavailable.
@container (width > 400px){.card{grid-template:...}}
v5_principal.the_realization: useMediaQuery is often the wrong question. Container queries say 'I respond to my container, not the viewport' — that's almost always what components actually want. Viewport-aware components are coupled to layout.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — works but hydration warnings
when_to_use_which_version: v3 for behavior, v4-v5 prefer CSS

scenario: Form spans 5 steps, validate per step, allow back

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Lose data on back, no validation, no URL sync, no resume after refresh

v2_mid_level.code: const [data,setData]=useState({});const update=(s)=>setData(d=>({...d,...s}));// Single state object, merge per step
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Refresh wipes everything, no per-step validation, no jumping to step

v3_senior.code:
// URL sync + sessionStorage persistence
const step=Number(useSearchParams().get('step')||0);useEffect(()=>{sessionStorage.setItem('wizard',JSON.stringify(data))},[data]);// Validation per step before next
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Step in URL (shareable, back-button works), persists across refresh, per-step validation gate

v4_staff.code:
// State machine — explicit transitions, guards, side effects
const machine=createMachine({initial:'step1',states:{step1:{on:{NEXT:{target:'step2',cond:'step1Valid'}},step2:{...}}}})
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Wizard logic in machine (testable, visualizable), can't reach invalid states, branching flows trivial

v5_principal.code:
// Wizard is a workflow. Backend-driven flow (server decides next step), allows A/B testing, skip logic, save-and-resume across devices.
const {currentStep,submit}=useWorkflow('signup-v3')
v5_principal.the_realization: Wizards seem like UI but are workflows. Once you put the flow in the backend, you can A/B test, personalize, skip steps based on data — without frontend changes. Hard-coded wizards are PMs waiting to file tickets.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — works locally, no resume
when_to_use_which_version: v3 always, v4 for complex flows, v5 for marketing-critical

feature_concept: Infinite scroll with restore on back
scenario: Load more as user scrolls; navigate to detail, come back, restore position

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Scroll handler fires 60x/sec, no observer, back returns to top, loses all loaded items

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Back-navigation loses everything, scroll position lost, no virtualization (1000 items = slow)

v3_senior.code:
// React Query useInfiniteQuery + sessionStorage scroll save
const {data,fetchNextPage}=useInfiniteQuery({queryKey:['items'],...});useEffect(()=>{const y=sessionStorage.getItem('scrollY');if(y)window.scrollTo(0,+y);return()=>sessionStorage.setItem('scrollY',String(window.scrollY))},[])
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Cache survives navigation, scroll restored, no observer leaks

v4_staff.code:
// Virtualized infinite: TanStack Virtual + useInfiniteQuery
const rv=useVirtualizer({count:flatData.length+1,...});// Trigger fetchNextPage when last item virtualized
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Renders only visible items (10k items, smooth), works with infinite query, scroll restore via virtualizer offset

v5_principal.code:
// RSC + Suspense for initial, client cache for subsequent. URL-based pagination cursor for shareable deep links. View Transitions for smooth detail navigation.
// Server: stream pages. Client: prefetch on hover.
v5_principal.the_realization: Infinite scroll is the most misused pattern. Often pagination/load-more is better UX (back button, focus, accessibility). When infinite is right, the hard parts are restoration and virtualization, not the trigger.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — works but back-button is destruction
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum, v4 for 1000+, v5 for feeds

scenario: Modal opens another modal; manage focus and z-index

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Focus trap stays on Modal1, escape closes both, backdrop click ambiguity

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Body scroll lock applied twice (no-op the second time but unlock too early), aria-hidden on background wrong

v3_senior.code:
// Use Radix Dialog / react-aria — they handle stacks, focus return, scroll lock refcount
<Dialog.Root open={open1}>...<Dialog.Root open={open2}>
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Libraries handle focus return (close Modal2 → focus back to Modal1, close Modal1 → focus back to opener), refcounted scroll lock

v4_staff.code:
// Question whether nesting is right. Often nested modal indicates flow problem — should be wizard, drawer-over-modal, or inline expand.
// If truly needed: shared portal target, z-index via stacking context, FLIP animation between
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Recognizes nested modals as UX smell, has design alternatives, when unavoidable handles z-index via stacking contexts not arbitrary numbers

v5_principal.code:
// Modal-as-route. Each modal a URL. Stack = URL hierarchy. Back button works. Deep-linkable. Reachable from notifications.
// /items/123/edit/confirm — three nested routes, each renders as modal
v5_principal.the_realization: Nested modals are usually wrong. When right, they're navigation, not components. Treating them as routes gives free back-button, deep linking, history — for less code than DIY stack management.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — focus is wrong, ships
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum, v4 question necessity, v5 for SaaS

scenario: Click column to sort; shift-click adds secondary sort

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Type-aware comparison missing, no nulls handling, no multi-column, mutates state on every render

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Single column only, no stable sort across columns

v3_senior.code:
// Multi-column sort with priority order
const sortCriteria:Sort[]=[{col:'date',dir:'desc'},{col:'name',dir:'asc'}];const sorted=useMemo(()=>[...data].sort((a,b)=>{for(const s of sortCriteria){const c=compare(a[s.col],b[s.col])*(s.dir==='desc'?-1:1);if(c!==0)return c}return 0}),[data,sortCriteria])
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Multi-column, stable, type-aware, locale-aware string compare

v4_staff.code:
// Server-side sort for large datasets, URL state
const params=new URLSearchParams();params.set('sort',sortCriteria.map(s=>`${s.dir==='desc'?'-':''}${s.col}`).join(','));const {data}=useQuery(['items',params.toString()],()=>fetch('/items?'+params))
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Server pushes work down (correct for >1000 rows), URL-shareable, locale handled server-side

v5_principal.code:
// Index-aware sort. Don't sort what backend already indexed by. Compose: server pre-sorts by index, client re-sorts only selection.
// For client-side: use TanStack Table — handles edge cases, types, accessibility (aria-sort)
v5_principal.the_realization: Sorting looks trivial — it isn't. Stability, types, nulls, locale, multi-column, ARIA, server-vs-client decision, index awareness. TanStack Table exists because every team rewrites this and gets it wrong.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — single column, ships
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum, v4 for >1k rows, v5 for huge datasets

scenario: Spotlight-style overlay searching commands and content

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Conflicts with browser, no platform check (Ctrl on Windows), no input focus, no fuzzy search

v2_mid_level.code: const isMac=navigator.platform.includes('Mac');const cmdKey=isMac?'metaKey':'ctrlKey';// Filter list with includes()
v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: Substring search misses fuzzy, no ranking, no grouping, no recent items, no keyboard nav

v3_senior.code:
// Fuse.js or @tanstack/match-sorter for fuzzy ranking
const results=useMemo(()=>matchSorter(items,query,{keys:['title','aliases']}),[query]);// Arrow keys move highlight, Enter executes
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Fuzzy match with typo tolerance, ranking, keyboard navigation, recent items via localStorage

v4_staff.code:
// cmdk library — built for this. Composable groups, async loading, animations, accessibility.
<Command><Command.Input/><Command.List><Command.Empty/><Command.Group heading='Actions'>{...}</Command.Group><Command.Group heading='Recent'>{...}</Command.Group></Command.List></Command>
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Battle-tested patterns, async groups (search content + commands), built-in keyboard, leverages composition

v5_principal.code:
// Command palette is product navigation. Extensible by features (each feature registers commands). Analytics on what users search. AI command (natural language → action).
registerCommand({id:'go-to-billing',title:'Go to billing',keywords:['payment','subscription'],run:()=>navigate('/billing')})
v5_principal.the_realization: Command palette done right is power-user UX and product navigation. Done wrong it's keyboard-shortcut-with-extra-steps. The architecture (extensible registry, analytics, AI) is what makes it transformative.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — opens, basic search
when_to_use_which_version: v3 for serious tools, v4+ leverage cmdk

scenario: User uploads phone photo (8MB); resize/compress before upload

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: 8MB photos, no preview, no rotation correction, no validation

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: EXIF orientation wrong, blocks main thread on huge files, no HEIC, color profile lost

v3_senior.code:
// browser-image-compression library handles EXIF, runs in worker
const compressed=await imageCompression(file,{maxWidthOrHeight:1600,maxSizeMB:1,useWebWorker:true})
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: EXIF orientation respected, Web Worker (non-blocking), size budget, HEIC handled

v4_staff.code:
// OffscreenCanvas + Web Worker for parallel processing. Progressive: client compress + server transcode (AVIF/WebP).
// AbortController for cancellation if user removes before upload completes.
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: True parallel (multiple workers), cancellable, modern formats, client + server pipeline

v5_principal.code:
// Image is a product asset. Pipeline: client (validate, resize, EXIF strip for privacy) → CDN (transcode AVIF/WebP/sizes) → component (srcset, lazy, LQIP).
// Privacy: strip GPS, anonymize. Watermark optional.
v5_principal.the_realization: Image upload spans privacy (EXIF GPS), performance (resize, lazy), correctness (orientation, color), formats (HEIC, AVIF), and CDN. Engineers who treat it as 'POST file' ship terrible UX or leak user GPS.

where_most_engineers_stop: v1 — uploads 8MB photos
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum, v4-v5 for production

scenario: Serve right size for device + DPR

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: Same 4MB image on mobile and desktop, blurry on retina

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: JPEG only (no WebP/AVIF), no LQIP, no lazy

v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: Format negotiation, lazy loading, layout-shift prevented (width/height), async decode

v4_staff.code:
// next/image or @unpic/react — on-demand transformation via CDN, automatic srcset, LQIP, AVIF/WebP
<Image src='/p.jpg' width={800} height={600} placeholder='blur' blurDataURL={lqip}/>
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: CDN handles all formats/sizes, LQIP for perceived speed, framework integration

v5_principal.code:
// Image is a CDN/build problem. Imgix/Cloudinary/Cloudflare Images. Art direction via <picture>. Container queries for component-local responsive (CSS image-set + container).
// Performance: critical images preloaded, others lazy with priority hints.
v5_principal.the_realization: Responsive images aren't a markup problem — they're a pipeline. CDN, transcoding, caching, format negotiation, preload hints. The browser API is one layer; the system is five.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — sizes work, no formats
when_to_use_which_version: v3 if no framework, v4 always with Next, v5 for media-heavy

scenario: User drags files onto area to upload

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: No hover state, no whole-page drop (drops outside zone open file), no folder support, no file type filter

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: DragLeave fires on child elements (flickers), no global prevention, no file validation

v3_senior.code:
// Counter approach to handle child enter/leave
const count=useRef(0);onDragEnter:()=>{count.current++;setHover(true)};onDragLeave:()=>{if(--count.current===0)setHover(false)};// Global preventDefault on window prevents drops outside zone
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: No flicker, global drop guard, file type/size validation, accessible button fallback

v4_staff.code:
// react-dropzone — solves all edge cases
const {getRootProps,getInputProps,isDragActive}=useDropzone({onDrop,accept:{'image/*':[]},maxSize:10*1024*1024})
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Library handles edge cases, accessible (click to select), keyboard, multiple files, paste support

v5_principal.code:
// Drop is one input among many: click, paste, drag, share target (PWA), camera. Unify as upload pipeline. Show transcoding/preview/progress consistently.
<UploadInput sources={['file','camera','paste','dragdrop']}/>
v5_principal.the_realization: Drop zones aren't a widget — they're one entry point to an upload flow. Camera, paste, share-target, drag, click — all same destination. Designing the pipeline once means consistent UX across inputs.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — flickers, ships
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum, v4+ for production

scenario: Show toasts; multiple toasts queue, auto-dismiss, stack

v1_junior.what_still_breaks: New toast cancels previous, no stacking, no actions, no swipe-dismiss, no severity

v2_mid_level.what_still_breaks: No screen reader announcement, no pause on hover, no swipe-dismiss, no action button

v3_senior.code:
{toasts.map(t=>
{t.message}
)}
;// onMouseEnter pause timer, onMouseLeave resume
v3_senior.what_makes_it_senior: aria-live announces, pause on hover, action button (undo pattern), severity types

v4_staff.code:
// react-hot-toast / sonner — handles all of the above + animation, stacking, swipe, focus return
import {toast,Toaster} from 'sonner';toast.success('Saved',{action:{label:'Undo',onClick:undo}})
v4_staff.what_makes_it_staff: Polished library, swipe gestures, FLIP animations, RTL support, themeable

v5_principal.the_realization: Toast vs modal vs banner vs inline isn't aesthetic — it's a hierarchy of attention. Pick the right channel for the message's importance and locality. Junior reaches for toast for everything.

where_most_engineers_stop: v2 — works, no a11y
when_to_use_which_version: v3 minimum, v4 use library, v5 for design system
